Title: Metabolic engineering provides insight into the regulation of thiamin biosynthesis in plants

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression analysis across single- and multigene overexpression lines; LC–MS/MS profiling of TMP, thiamin, and TPP
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TH2 expression was monitored to test whether TMP-to-thiamin conversion limits B1 accumulation. 2. No clear correlation between TH2 expression and thiamin accumulation was observed. 3. High thiamin accumulation occurred in multigene lines even when TH2 expression decreased, indicating endogenous TH2 activity is sufficient and not limiting.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TH2 is not limiting for thiamin accumulation under the tested engineering conditions; native activity supports conversion of TMP to thiamin.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Thiamin (vitamin B1 vitamers: TMP, thiamin, TPP)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor metabolism; thiamin pyrophosphate supports crucial carbon metabolism reactions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in seedlings; transcript abundance relatively similar to THIC and TPK in WT, much higher than TH1 according to supplemental transcript comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 15-d-old complete seedlings grown in vitro on half-strength MS + sucrose
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15-d-old seedlings grown on half-strength Murashige and Skoog medium with 10 g L−1 sucrose; sampled mid-light period
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS; RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a monitored endogenous pathway component, but not directly engineered because downstream of TMP was intentionally not targeted. Authors conclude biofortification approaches would not benefit from implementing TH2 overexpression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target for B1 biofortification in this system because TH2 activity was sufficient and not rate-limiting.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR measuring combined TPK1/TPK2 expression; LC–MS/MS measurement of TPP and total B1 in engineered lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Combined TPK1/TPK2 expression was assessed as a potential downstream bottleneck in B1 engineering. 2. THIC overexpression did not increase TPP despite no notable TPK induction, but multigene lines still achieved modest TPP increases without TPK engineering. 3. Endogenous TPK activity was concluded sufficient and not limiting for B1 accumulation in Arabidopsis seedlings.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native TPK activity supports modest TPP increases; TPK is not a limiting step for total B1 accumulation in this study.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: TPP is the bioactive cofactor form required for central carbon metabolism
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in WT seedlings; transcript abundance relatively similar to THIC and TH2 according to supplemental comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 15-d-old complete seedlings grown in vitro on half-strength MS + sucrose
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15-d-old seedlings grown on half-strength Murashige and Skoog medium with 10 g L−1 sucrose; sampled mid-light period
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS; RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly state biofortification approaches would likely not benefit from adding TPK implementation, consistent with prior rice leaf data cited.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority engineering target for thiamin biofortification in this context.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Thiamin pyrimidine biosynthesis enzyme; HMP-P synthase
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: HMP-P leading to total HMP pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of the pyrimidine moiety HMP-P for thiamin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for B1 vitamers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point, but insufficient alone to strongly raise total B1 under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; RT-qPCR; LC–MS/MS quantification of HMP/HMP-related metabolites and B1 vitamers; single- and multigene comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THIC overexpression strongly elevated THIC transcript and caused massive accumulation of pyrimidine intermediates, with total HMP up to ~40-fold higher than WT. 2. Despite strong precursor accumulation, THIC alone had limited impact on thiamin and no notable increase in TPP, indicating downstream limitation, especially thiazole supply. 3. In multigene lines, higher transgenic THIC expression often coincided with higher thiamin accumulation. 4. Excess pyrimidine accumulation was associated with abnormal growth on soil, highlighting need for branch balancing and detoxification/salvage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong pyrimidine intermediate accumulation and contributes to thiamin increase only when balanced with other pathway steps; alone is insufficient for major B1 enhancement.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B1/TPP supports essential carbon metabolism and human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seedlings; endogenous THIC is under riboswitch feedback and circadian promoter control
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15-d-old seedlings grown on half-strength Murashige and Skoog medium with 10 g L−1 sucrose; sampled mid-light period; soil growth revealed abnormal phenotype in some THIC overexpressors
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS; RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess pyrimidine accumulation associated with abnormal growth on soil and likely toxicity from pyrimidine breakdown products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Native THIC is controlled by a 3′-UTR TPP riboswitch and circadian promoter regulation; the engineering rationale emphasized bypassing native feedback. Authors recommend balancing pyrimidine with thiazole supply and salvage/detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important engineering target, but should be stacked with thiazole-branch support and salvage/detoxification rather than overexpressed alone.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Thiamin thiazole biosynthesis enzyme; suicidal HET-P synthase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream branch step / rate-limiting control point
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: HET-P leading to total HET pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of the thiazole moiety HET-P for thiamin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for B1 vitamers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting / major control point under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; RT-qPCR; LC–MS/MS measurement of HET/HET-related metabolites and B1 vitamers; single- and multigene comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. THI1 overexpression significantly increased THI1 transcript and strongly elevated thiazole intermediates, especially nonphosphorylated HET. 2. Among single-gene strategies, THI1 overexpression most effectively increased B1 vitamers, including TMP, thiamin, and TPP. 3. THI1 activity was identified as a rate-limiting control point for B1 biosynthetic flux in Arabidopsis seedlings. 4. High HET accumulation exposed inefficiency of endogenous thiazole salvage, suggesting salvage enhancement could further strengthen B1 engineering. 5. The suicidal nature of THI1 likely constrains thiazole supply and imposes energetic cost.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases thiazole intermediate supply and most effectively boosts B1 vitamers among single-gene interventions; key determinant of pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B1/TPP supports essential carbon metabolism and human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly abundant transcript in WT seedlings relative to other thiamin genes; expressed in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15-d-old seedlings grown on half-strength Murashige and Skoog medium with 10 g L−1 sucrose; sampled mid-light period
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS; RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Energetically expensive suicidal enzyme; accumulation of nonphosphorylated HET indicates inefficient salvage and possible branch inefficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose replacing suicidal THI1 with nonsuicidal variants from grasses or improved engineered enzymes as a major future engineering opportunity. Additional HET salvage and transport capacity may be needed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top-priority engineering target for B1 biofortification; likely best deployed with THIC, TH1, and improved salvage or nonsuicidal THI1 variants.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Intermediate condensation step linking pyrimidine and thiazole branches
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Pyrimidine and thiazole intermediates (HMP-P/HMP-PP and HET-P)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes phosphorylation/activation of the pyrimidine moiety and condensation of pyrimidine and thiazole intermediates to form TMP.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate B1 vitamer precursor/product TMP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not limiting alone, but beneficial when combined with THIC and THI1 stimulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in Arabidopsis; RT-qPCR; LC–MS/MS quantification of TMP, thiamin, TPP and intermediates; comparison of TH1 single-gene and TT versus TTT multigene lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TH1 overexpression strongly increased TH1 transcript but had limited effect on overall B1 accumulation in single-gene lines. 2. The most evident single-gene effect was increased TMP, the direct downstream product. 3. TH1 was not rate-limiting on its own. 4. Additional TH1 overexpression in TTT lines improved thiamin accumulation compared with TT lines, indicating benefit when precursor supply from THIC and THI1 is elevated.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alone mainly raises TMP modestly; in combination with THIC and THI1 helps achieve higher thiamin and total B1.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Vitamin B1/TPP supports essential carbon metabolism and human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low transcript abundance in WT seedlings relative to THIC, TH2, TPK, and especially THI1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 15-d-old seedlings grown on half-strength Murashige and Skoog medium with 10 g L−1 sucrose; sampled mid-light period
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC–MS/MS; RT-qPCR; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude natural thiamin biosynthesis requires only very low TH1 expression, but extra TH1 becomes beneficial under boosted precursor supply. Some increased nonphosphorylated intermediates in TH1 lines may reflect breakdown rather than productive flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supportive stacking target with THIC and THI1 rather than standalone engineering target.
